articles.title: Burnham pauses plan to release some prisoners early from September
articles.summary: The prime minister says no prisoners will be released until a review has been conducted.

articles.title: Mum and daughter who died at beach were trying to save boy, 7
articles.summary: Shally Rahman and teenager Samiha Rahman were pulled from the water, emergency services say.

articles.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
articles.summary: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

articles.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
articles.summary: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

articles.title: PM to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
articles.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street.

articles.title: Rayner rules out repaying severance cash after making cabinet return
articles.summary: The returning housing secretary received £16,876 after leaving government in September 2025.

articles.title: UK 'ready to defend itself' after Iran says bases are 'legitimate targets'
articles.summary: An Iranian military group has issued a warning to the UK over allowing US forces to use British bases for strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Gary Lineker among millionaires asking Andy Burnham to tax them more
articles.summary: A group including the former England footballer write to the new PM telling him "we can afford it".

articles.title: US lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
articles.summary: A new bill would let the US government order the shutdown AI models that pose a major public threat.

articles.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to psychiatric hospital
articles.summary: Rudakubana was jailed for 52 years in 2025 for the murder of three young girls at a dance class.

articles.title: Pubs cheer business rates relief while other firms 'left out in the cold'
articles.summary: Pubs welcomed the support package, while other High Street firms asked why they were left out.

articles.title: Airport drop off fees up by a third - here's how to avoid unexpected charges
articles.summary: The cost of parking closest to the terminal has hit £10 at two of the UK's airports.

articles.title: What could happen to prisoner early release scheme now PM has paused it?
articles.summary: The PM's decision comes after a police officer's widow said the possible early release of his killers was "deplorable".

articles.title: Frank Gardner: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
articles.summary: The Iran-backed group in Yemen has threatened to target Saudi vessels in a vital shipping route.

articles.title: Andy Burnham's plan to collaborate with social media creators
articles.summary: The new prime minister plans to work with influencers and social media creators to get his message out.

articles.title: Trump's nuclear deal with Saudis jettisons longstanding US demands
articles.summary: Signing a nuclear pact in the Middle East is a bold move that has drawn criticism in the US and in Israel.

articles.title: Wildfires and record dry conditions trigger drought in parts of Wales
articles.summary: Wales has seen about 5% of the average rainfall for July, after a series of back-to-back heatwaves.

articles.title: Police failed to check Manchester synagogue attacker's phones despite arresting him twice
articles.summary: Greater Manchester Police apologises because Jihad Al-Shamie's devices were not reviewed before the attack.

articles.title: Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes
articles.summary: The Iran-backed group, which previously announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia, also claims to have hit another vessel.

articles.title: BBC's Fake or Fortune uncovers 'enormously important' painting worth £100,000
articles.summary: The painting by the 18th Century Scottish artist Katherine Read is thought to be worth more than £100,000.

articles.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
articles.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

articles.title: Love Island: All Stars winners Molly Smith and Tom Clare marry
articles.summary: The couple won the first series of the spin-off edition of the hit dating show in 2024.

articles.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
articles.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.

articles.summary: Arsenal are set to make a £70m offer for Newcastle captain Bruno Guimaraes.

articles.title: Defoe leaves Woking after 'eye-opening' first managerial role
articles.summary: Former England and Tottenham striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his departure from National League side Woking after just four months and nine matches in charge.

articles.title: Italy confirm Ancelotti approach before Guardiola
articles.summary: Italy held discussions with Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before opening talks with Pep Guardiola.

articles.title: Can Emery work his magic again at Villa?

articles.title: 'What's happened there?!' - Buttler and Dawson's moment to forget
articles.summary: Manchester Super Giants fielders Jos Buttler and Liam Dawson inadvertently teach a vital lesson in communication after a huge fielding mix-up, missing a simple catch that would have removed London Spirit's Kiran Carlson in the men's Hundred.
